runs.text: Control room operators must record critical information quickly while keeping their language clear and calm. The caller may be distressed, so every typed note should capture the location, the immediate danger, the people involved, and any changes that happen while help is being arranged. Type steadily, correct mistakes where needed, and continue until the timer ends.

A female caller reports suspicious activity near a row of parked vehicles behind a block of flats on Green Lane. She saw a male walking slowly between the cars. He was shining a torch through several windows and looking inside the vehicles. The male is described as white, around thirty years old, and wearing a dark hooded jacket with grey tracksuit bottoms. The caller says he stopped beside a silver van and pulled on the rear door handle. When another resident entered the car park, the male quickly walked away. The caller has not seen any damage. She believes the male may be trying to steal items from inside cars. The male was last seen walking towards the nearby underpass, carrying a black backpack.

A caller reports a road traffic collision at the junction of Bridge Road and Elm Avenue. The incident involves a motorcycle and a small delivery van. The caller says the motorcycle was travelling quickly before it collided with the side of the van as the van turned into the junction. The rider has fallen onto the pavement and appears to have injuries to his arm and shoulder. He is conscious, but he is in pain and struggling to move. Several members of the public have gathered nearby; traffic is now building in both directions. The van driver is visibly shaken. He remains at the scene and is speaking with witnesses. The caller says fuel appears to be leaking from the motorcycle onto the road surface.

A caller reports a possible domestic incident inside a neighbouring flat at Kingston Court. Loud shouting has been heard for approximately twenty minutes: one male voice, one female voice, and intermittent crying. The female has repeatedly shouted, "Leave me alone," and "Get out of my flat." Moments later, the caller heard a loud crash, possibly glass breaking, followed by silence for several seconds. The argument then resumed. The male has been heard threatening to damage property, accusing the female of calling police, and saying that "things will get worse" if anyone comes to the door. The caller believes a young child may also be inside the address; crying can occasionally be heard in the background.
